core_payload.content:
# A2C Readiness Control

## 1. Role
A2C Readiness Control owns the pre-mutation control loop that decides whether the refinery is allowed to begin. It verifies runtime readiness, classifies the workspace, and scores intake risk before any stage work is permitted to touch governed memory.

## 2. Readiness Control Surface
This sub-hub groups `agent-activation-readiness`, `workspace-recon`, and `deep-intake-investigation`. Together they define whether the A2C skill surface is live, which SSOT ladder governs the repository, and how duplicate, conflict, PII, scale, or routing risk should shape the packet before ingestion proceeds.

## 3. Operating Boundary
This sub-hub does not capture raw material, construct capsule candidates, or emit graph mutations. Those responsibilities belong to `material-preparation`, `candidate-construction`, `governance-handoffs`, and `admission-emission`.

neuro_concentrate.summary: A2C Readiness Control groups the pre-mutation control surfaces of the refinery: Agent Activation Readiness, Workspace Recon, and Deep Intake Investigation. It keeps skill readiness, repository classification, SSOT ladder discovery, and intake risk scoring together so A2C can refuse unsafe packets before raw material ever enters the stage line. This boundary matters because the cheapest ingestion mistake is the one prevented before the first mutation, and readiness law should stay explicit rather than dissolving into later stage mechanics.

epistemic_ledger.rationale: Minted during A2C decomposition to isolate readiness law, workspace classification, and intake risk scoring from stage mechanics and later governance handoff.
